notes: Confirmed: SKCanvas.DrawSurface and SKSurface.Draw lack SKSamplingOptions overloads across all tested versions (3.116.0, 3.119.2, main). In contrast, SKCanvas.DrawImage has 4 overloads accepting SKSamplingOptions. The upstream Skia C++ API supports SkSamplingOptions on SkSurface::draw but this was never exposed through the C API shim or C# bindings. Workaround: use SKSurface.Snapshot() + SKCanvas.DrawImage with SKSamplingOptions. PR #3491 is open to add the missing overloads.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console project with reporter's SkiaSharp version (3.116.0)
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3344 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3344 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.116.0
reproductionSteps.output: Package 'SkiaSharp' is compatible with all the specified frameworks in project.

filesCreated.description: Console app using reflection to enumerate DrawSurface/Draw/DrawImage overloads and check for SKSamplingOptions parameter presence, plus workaround test via Snapshot + DrawImage

versionResults.notes: SKCanvas.DrawSurface has 2 overloads (none with SKSamplingOptions). SKSurface.Draw has 1 overload (no SKSamplingOptions). DrawImage has 4 overloads with SKSamplingOptions for comparison.
Same gap persists on latest stable. No SKSamplingOptions overloads on DrawSurface or SKSurface.Draw.
Main branch source still lacks SKSamplingOptions overloads. The C API shim (sk_surface_draw) only wraps the convenience overload without sampling. PR #3491 is open to address this.

actionability.reason: Valid enhancement confirmed across all versions including main. PR #3491 is open and implementing the fix. The gap exists because the C API shim only exposes the convenience SkSurface::draw overload without SkSamplingOptions, and the C# bindings were never updated to match the DrawImage pattern.

actions.description: Reclassify as enhancement since this is a missing overload, not broken behavior
Acknowledge the gap, provide workaround, and reference the open PR
Link to open PR implementing the fix

workarounds: Use SKSurface.Snapshot() to get an SKImage, then call SKCanvas.DrawImage with SKSamplingOptions — this provides full sampling control with a small overhead for the snapshot

corrections.corrected: type/enhancement — this is a missing API overload, not broken behavior. Existing DrawSurface works correctly with default sampling.
